title: westringia rupicola
intro: Westringia rupicola, also known as the rocky coast rosemary or mountain rosemary, is a shrub native to Australia, characterized by its small white or purple flowers and a woody, evergreen habit. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Westringia rupicola include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and exhibit antioxidant properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effects. Westringia rupicola can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or orally, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
